models.official_description: The ARCHANGEL is power with purpose, Reborn from Kolby Aipa’s Hitman — the board that carried his rhythm: fast, fluid, and fearless — this is the shape he gravitated toward for everything from playful peaks to punchy beach breaks. In Scripture, an Archangel is a chief messenger and warrior of light — a divine being who moves with authority and leads heaven’s charge. That same energy lives in this design. The Archangel is an intuitive daily driver built for surgical surfing in small to medium waves, with a sweet spot in that chest- to head-high zone. Ideal for threading beach breaks and teeing off on oncoming sections, it features a Southern California–inspired rocker, staged bottom contour, and full rails that keep it lively yet grounded. It moves with intention underfoot — light on entry, locked through turns, and explosive when it’s time to commit. THE ARCHANGEL “For the Lord Himself will come down from heaven, with a loud command, with the voice of the archangel and with the trumpet call of God, and the dead in Christ will rise first.” — 1 Thessalonians 4:16 (NIV)

models.official_description: The Ben Aipa Fish has been a staple board from Ben's archive of design dating back to 1971. This board has been ever evolving throughout the decades to meet the progression of various surfers abilities. The B.A. Fish ( which we've been calling the B.A.D. FISH the past few years) combines the classic Ben Aipa Fish with some modern performance adjustments by Duke. Notably, the BA Fish was originally designed with a large swallow tail, which we now offer as a primary option for the OG feeling. This model is available as a, quad, twin or also as a hybrid twin +2 fin box set up.

models.official_description: A favored model for the 210 pounds plus surfer, the Big Boy Sting is designed for the big surfers who want to maximize power without giving up maneuverability and is also ideal for longboarders looking to step down towards a shortboard. Designed and Proven to perform by Ben Aipa on the South and North shores of Oahu in the 90's, The Big Boy Sting underwent heavy testing through the ‘2000s. After decades of refinement and 1000’s of boards built, the Big Boy Sting is now considered the most iconic Aipa design for the plus sized surfer. The wide forward surface area concentrating into a shortboard-esque nose, with generous foam in the center, tapered rails and the iconic STING element that drastically reduces surface area in the rear, allows the Big Boy Sting to paddle like a longboard, yet turn tight and fast in the pocket like a shortboard. The Big Boy Sting can easily handle 1-8' surf, and To ensure the board works in multiple conditions, we’ve included a 4+1 fin setup, so surfers can go with a quad fin setup for down the line speed and looseness or a 2 +1 for extra control in more powerful waves. The Big Boy Sting is a high-performance heavy surfer’s precision weapon. For the first time ever, the Big Boy Sting is now available in Surftech’s Fusion HD construction. A lightweight, durable epoxy build that adds extra liveliness to an already energetic design.

models.official_description: Ben Aipa’s plus sized staple performance longboard, The Big Brother Sting is a high-performance tank designed to power through the mushiest of conditions. Extra thickness in the center of the board with a nicely foiled rail gives the perfect balance of float and performance. Riders will be able to catch any wave without sacrificing maneuverability. A wider outline up in the front of the board increases stability, while the AIPA STING element drastically reduces surface area in the rear allowing for quick directional changes. The Big Brother Sting can easily handle 1-10' surf, and to ensure the board works in multiple conditions, we’ve included a 4+1 fin setup, so surfers can go with a quad fin setup for down the line speed and looseness or a 2 +1 for extra control in more powerful waves. To keep things light, we’ve built the Big Brother Sting in Surftech’s Fusion HD construction. A lightweight and durable epoxy with a lively flex. Big stoke comes guaranteed with the Big Brother Sting; finally, a board that makes less than ideal conditions just as fun as the best day of the year.

models.official_description: "In nature , the Bonefish is the fastest fish in the ocean that loves shallow waters.. The BONE FISH was designed to be that hyper-reactive and speedy design that fits in tight pockets and excels in punchy shorebreaks or reef breaks. As the next step in the evolution of the fish, our goal with the BONEFISH was to harness the curves of the proven concept and create a more aggressive fish inspired performance driven shape for when you want to have that shortboard feel in a smaller fish-like package."

models.official_description: DA BUTTZ is an homage to the carefree spirit of original Team Aipa surfer, Buttons Kaluhiokalani. Buttons was known for innovating insane, never seen before maneuvers in the 70’s, such as the carving 360. Originally a kneeboard design, in 1976, Ben Aipa modified DA BUTTZ as a compact groveler style funboard. The modifications made the board faster and looser than antiquated Fishes of the era, which made it possible for Uncle Buttons to play around with skate style antics on small SouthShore waves. From ankle to head high fun this is the one. We consider DA BUTTZ to be the ideal fish alternative that shares a similar short squatty concept with a ton of volume packed in, and flat rocker characteristics of a fish, but is enhanced by the Sting design element and outrageously wide swallow tail for responsive and unique fun performance feeling, that makes those weaker small days feel lively. DA BUTTZ boasts a classic Ben Aipa bottom contour progression from Roll, vee to double concave with vee out the tail, incorporating the hydroplane speed boat inspired “Bevel Rails”. A 45 degree split rail design element that provides increased lift/speed, rail to rail reduced surface area for heightened performance, and positive rail control for carves. When the rest of the crowd is being overly serious on their shortboards and mid-lengths, DA BUTTZ will keep your surfing playful and alive!

models.official_description: A legend reborn. Da Sting is the one surfboard in all of history that, when you see it—you know exactly where it came from. Inspired by Ben Aipa’s observation of a hydroplane speed boat in 1974, Da Sting carries forward his iconic outline—unchanged—and balances it with a clean, modern feel underfoot. Since its inception, Ben shaped countless iterations of the Sting. But this one? This is the version Buttons Kaluhiokalani and Mark Liddell were, in Ben’s own words, “smashing, banging, and innovating” on the South Shore of O?ahu in the summer of ’75. It was during this explosive time that the now-iconic photo of da boys standing on the rock wall at Kaisers was taken—later landing on the cover of Surfer Magazine in 1976. That cover shot, as Ben said, “went viral,” catapulting the Sting to global recognition. Da Sting blends the classic with the contemporary, giving surfers a chance to ride a piece of history while feeling modern performance under their feet. It’s not about specs—it’s about something deeper. This is a legacy you can ride. History you can feel. A tribute to the spark that ignited the performance revolution and set the surfing world on fire. Feel the energy of the ’70s. Surf the board that lit the fuse. Experience Da Sting.

models.official_description: Every quiver needs a sleeper. A board you can send into the pit and trust—one that bites hard when you throw yourself over a shorebreak ledge. This is ours. Originally developed in parallel with the Dark Twinn as its quad-fin alter ego, the DARK HORSE has stepped out of the shadows to claim its own identity in the Alternative Performance category. We pulled in the nose, increased the rocker, and reimagined the overall flow of the outline into a clean round tail—giving this thoroughbred the control and responsiveness to hit critical sections, drive hard off the bottom, and release on command. It still carries the AIPA wing philosophy of surface area reduction, with the wing strategically tuned to complement the 5-fin setup. Ride it quad, twin + trailer, or thruster—depending on the feel you’re chasing. The DARK HORSE is a daily driver built for the hardcore underdogs who want to surf aggressively in 3–5 foot hollow waves. Fast and Reactive.

models.official_description: When I designed the Dark Twinn, I was chasing a blend of timeless style and futuristic feel. Inspired by my dad’s late ’70s winged twins and the smooth curves of his 90s thumb-tail swing templates, this board is all about flow, control, and attitude. The goal? Build an all-around board with a split personality—something that could grovel in small surf, but still light it up when it’s head high and firing. The Dark Twinn is made for the mischievous surfer who wants one board to do it all—and maybe cause a little trouble along the way. The bottom contour runs a toned-down version of Pops’ Pro-Am design—deep vee and double concave forming a high , softened spine under the back foot, like a racing clutch. It’s responsive, sensitive, and quick to move when you are. It got its name in the shaping bay. Under the lights, the double concaves threw shadows down the stringer—two deep lanes of light and dark. That’s why it ends with two N’s. Two distinct shadows. Two personalities. One obsession. Pro -Surfer, Zeke Lau said "I love my Dark Twinn. It's easy to maneuver and feels lively under my feet... If you want to go out there...have fun and come in happier, ride this thing".

models.official_description: Originally designed by Ben Aipa in 1976, the Flared Pin Twin returns with a modern twist but its soul remains untouched. This is a shape built for power and precision, anchored by Ben’s signature Pro-Am bottom a deep double concave that forms a high spine through the center. That spine acts almost like a built-in rudder, helping tame the looseness of the twin-fin setup. Same idea surfers chase today with a stubby trailer fin but Ben was doing it with foam and flow. The outline blends the hold of a pin tail with a subtle flare through the hips, giving it extra drive and a clean release off the tail. It’s smooth under pressure and electric when you let it loose. Designed for surfers who want twin-fin speed with confident hold in punchier conditions, the Flared Pin Twin walks that perfect line between retro cool and high-performance function.

models.official_description: The Genesis 1:1 Kolby once said, “Look at what our God made for us! He made this for us to enjoy.” This is where it all begins — creation in motion, the foundation of faith, flow, and explosive joy. Just as the verse speaks of the first act of creation, and of Kolby’s awakening in the Spirit, The Genesis 1:1 represents new beginnings for AIPA — celebrating the eternal life of Kolby Aipa and inspiring others to discover a new level of awareness in the happiness we are called to receive. The Genesis 1:1 is a refined all-around shortboard designed to excel in everyday surf. With a performance rocker, soft freestyle rails, and a sleek transitional single concave, it offers quick acceleration and effortless transitions. Its outline blends forgiveness with drive — delivering stability on entry, crisp response through turns, and just the right amount of release when it’s time to open up the throttle. Made for surfers seeking a board that feels intuitive, balanced, and alive, The Genesis 1:1 invites you to start every session with purpose and gratitude. It’s more than the first model in the KA Freestyle line — it’s a daily reminder that everything begins with faith and ends in gratitude. “In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.” — Genesis 1:1 (NIV)

models.official_description: The Ichthys, or fish symbol, is an ancient Christian emblem representing “Jesus Christ, Son of God, Savior”—an acronym derived from the Greek word ichthys (ἰχθύς), meaning “fish.” The ICHTHYS is a symbol of faith, the miracles of the Gospels, and the deep identity of those who follow Christ. A symbol. A statement. The ICHTHYS is the KA performance fish inspired by Kolby’s freestyle nature—crafted for the days you don’t want to surf so seriously… and just want to flow freely in the ocean God made for us to play in with your brothers and sisters of the salt. No stress, all joy. Born to honor the Son of God, this modern take on the classic AIPA fish responds with conviction in everything from playful runners to punchy walls. With its sleek performance outline, tuned rail line for acceleration, embedded double concave under the forward foot, and shallow fish tail for lift and control—you’ll feel immediate connection, explosive speed, and the freedom to lay down turns with purpose. For shortboarders who’ve never tapped into fish-style surfing, the ICHTHYS breaks the barrier—colliding two worlds in one sacred shape. Every KA model carries a message. The ICHTHYS says: Stand with me. And surf with the spirit of Christ. “Be strong and courageous. Do not be frightened, and do not be dismayed, for the LORD your God is with you wherever you go"” — Joshua 1:9

models.official_description: The KAISER is inspired by a board Ben Aipa shaped in 1976 for the playful waves of the South Shore of O?ahu. While Ben is best known for crafting powerful boards for powerful surfers, what most don’t realize is that Pops was desigining and riding short, stubby shapes in small surf long before grovelers were even a thing. Named after Kaisers—his go-to South Shore test spot (and the Latin word for Caesar)—this board channels Ben’s experimental and royal spirit. It’s a small-wave weapon with hidden volume, clean entry, and built-in speed. Fast and playful when the waves are soft, with the character to hold its own when the energy steps up. Big guys, small days, or a little more juice? No problem—the KAISER holds court.

models.official_description: The KILLSWITCH is a modern alternative shortboard built for surfers who don’t fit the typical shortboard mold. Designed for the 40+ crew with athletic builds and years of surf under their belt and for heavier, weekend-only warriors who need to make their sessions count! It’s a deceiving design—at first glance, it looks like a high-performance blade. But hidden inside that sleek outline is big braddah volume, strategically packed for power and flow. You get the wave count of a midelngth with the response of a shortboard. No bulk. No bog. Just clean lines, easy speed, and tight turning radius when you put it on rail. The KILLSWITCH is about reigniting your connection to high-performance surfing—no matter what size you are or how often you surf. It’s your permission to paddle out with confidence, flip the switch, and go.

models.official_description: Before the name AIPA was known worldwide, Ben was already changing the shape of how things were. The MAKAHA was shaped in 1965—just one year into his shaping journey—under the Surfboards Makaha label. While most boards of the era were bloated and bland, this one was sleek, refined, and decades ahead of its time. This wasn’t just a new shape—it was a disruption. The outline was an elegant and agressive-- a forward minded masterpiece. While others were still stuck in reverse softening rails for safety and ease, Ben was moving forward adding edges. He introduced tucked-under bottom rail lines with bite toward the tail—at a time when no one else was even thinking that way—because he wanted to push his surfing harder. The MAKAHA was a performance nose rider before that even had a name. It laid the foundation for what would become Ben’s groundbreaking MLB (Modern Longboard) design in the early ’80s, where he fused shortboard response with longboards aging soul and changed the game for good. This isn’t just a board—this the essence of a young Aipa who wasn’t interested in blending in. The MAKAHA is pure instinct, raw edge, and the first sign of a legend in the making.

models.official_description: In 1981, while the rest of the surf world was caught up in the shortboard thruster revolution, Ben Aipa flipped the script. He brought the longboard back—but not as you knew it. He engineered a new kind of weapon: the Modern Longboard—a high-performance tanker that moved like a shortboard and changed the way people thought abo ut riding longboards forever. Then in 1982, he doubled down and spun the idea into something even more disruptive: The MINI-TANK. A compact version of the Modern Longboard, ranging from 5’10” to 7’6”, designed to pack longboard flow into a more aggressive, agile frame. The MINI-TANK wasn’t just ahead of the curve—it was the curve. A true hybrid/fun board before those categories even had a name. Full outline for paddle power and trim, extra foam under the chest to keep your wave count high, and the rail-to-rail response of a high-performance shortboard—so you can lay it on rail and crack the lip when it counts. Quad for speed. Thruster for familiar control. Single fin for style. Twin plus trailer for freedom. Fin setups? Run the whole gambit. This board is for surfers who want to break the rules. (You know da rules… lol.)

models.official_description: Born in 1974. Rebuilt for the mod-vintage movement. The Modern Sting takes Ben Aipa’s iconic STING design—one that’s been evolving to execute in every era of surfing for half a century—and brings it into today with a transformed aesthetic and performance-tuned outline. Wide up front for speed, aggressively pulled in behind the Sting wing for fast release and tight control, combined with today’s rocker, rails, and bottom contours—this shape changed the face of surfing once, and it's still pushing boundaries today. From single fin flow to playful twin or twin plus trailer, to quad speed/liveliness and dominating thruster carves... you can run it how you want: single, twin, twin + trailer, quad, or thruster. It’s that versatile—and we’re stoked to share with you each sensation these setups can offer. Tested in 2024 by Dave Rastovich in Stab’s Electric Acid Surfboard Test and selected as one of the top three boards of the year, the Modern Sting is as relevant today as it was 50 years ago—now with decades of progression under the hood. Distinctive. Disruptive. 100% AIPA. Ben once said, “In order for surfing to change, surfboards need to change.” So… experience it, get stung, and be the next radical crew of surfers to carry on the movement that legends like Larry Bertlemann, Buttons Kaluhiokalani, Dane Kealoha, Mark Liddell, and Mark Richards started in the ’70s.

models.official_description: The Modern Swallow carries forward Ben Aipa’s original 1972 swallowtail design into the modern era. It’s a fusion of classic aesthetics and contemporary performance—made for surfers who respect the roots of high-performance surfing while seeking modern functionality. With a slightly filled-in, smoothed-out vintage outline, low apex rails, and a generous vee-to-double concave out the tail, the Modern Swallow delivers a balanced ride—speed, control, and smooth transitions from waist-high peelers to overhead punch. Designed to be ridden as a quad, it’s also wide open for experimentation. Twin, twinzer, 2+1, thruster, or single fin—this board adapts to your vibe and the waves in front of you. The Modern Swallow is a true expression of AIPA innovation and progression—honoring the past while carving toward the future.

models.official_description: The Peacemaker: is the step-up built for chaos — a board that brings calm, confidence, and clarity when the ocean turns powerful and unpredictable. Inspired by the words of Jesus — “Blessed are the peacemakers” — this model is designed to steady your mind and anchor your faith in challenging conditions. Previously known as the Alchemist (with the name changed to more apporitaley honor Kolby's beliefs), the Peacemaker was Kolby's go to step up for precision drops. With a proper big-wave rocker, foam forward, refined foiling in the tail section, a clean, controlled outline, and staged bottom contour ( roll-single-embedded double with a slight vee out the tail), The Peacemaker holds its line with authority.. Proven from Nicaragua, to China Walls, Pipeline and Teeahupo'... So, Where fear rises, The Peacemaker settles you. Where uncertainty hits, it responds with control. Where the wave demands commitment, this board delivers purpose. “Blessed are the peacemakers, for they will be called children of God.” — Matthew 5:9 (NIV)

Imagined as an inside joke by team rider Joel Bishop, the Retro Bishop combines the speed and flow of the BA Fish with the instinct for attack of the Bishop model. It’s loose when you want it, and locked-in when you need it. This is your go-to for speed, flow, and a little extra attitude. One board. Two personalities. All gas.

models.official_description: The Step Twin is an extension of the Dark Twinn—a split personality for when the waves turn on and gets a little heavier. Where the Dark Twinn maxes out, the Step Twin steps in and takes over. Designed for surfers who love the Dark Twinn's speed and spontaneity, but need more control and confidence when conditions call for it. This is your go-to in average to above-average surf when that extra hold and power becomes critical. Carrying the same rebellious identity, the Step Twin stretches the design into a more refined, dominant form, with a balanced arc outline that screams, “lay into me.” It’s still fast, playful, and loose—but now backed by added bite, and composure. Taking notes from our step up models, we designed in a touch more foam up front for early entry, boxed up the rails behind the wing and pulled the fins back for more precise power off the bottom, which perfectly complements that signature twin fin release off the top. Perfect for point breaks, reef setups, or punchy beaches—anywhere the Dark Twinn might tap out, the Step Twin comes out swinging.

models.official_description: Built for speed. Tuned for chaos. Designed to detonate 1–3' surf. Since Ben Aipa’s 1974 creation of the STING, AIPA has been on the forefront of performance designs. Duke continues to evolve his fathers designs by implementing the STING’s design principles of surface area reduction and control. Duke’s passion is collaborating with athletes from different backgrounds,so when Surf n’ Show’s Noel Salas approached Duke with a concept to co-design a small wave specific High-Performance Groveler, a design which he felt was missing in most surfboard quivers, Duke was 100% in to prove it could be done. The sole purpose for this model was to absolutely shred and traverse at Mach 2 speeds in 1’-3’ surf on a surfboard that’s not a fish, or a hybrid, but a true HP Groveler! It sounds cliche, but we truly focused on creating the fastest, most maneuverable board in its class. The Supernova defines this category with the focus of small wave ripping for surfers that like a performance outline under their feet without the restrictions of a wide nose fish or stigma of a typical hybrid board groveler. We incorporated 50-plus years of AIPA design history and Noel's career of testing 100s of surfboards in varying shapes into this board. We are not talking about a daily driver or all arounder that also kind of works in smaller waves like most board builders make. We are talking about a high-performance outline that works specifically for the small stuff, no fluff…. Duke said, “The SUPERNOVA represents the most intense and critical collaborative design work I’ve ever done. Even when the board was working unreal, we kept pushing the design, polishing it into the fastest, most responsive board in its class.. point blank”. For speed, the foundation of the board demanded we create the straightest rail line possible in a compact outline, and complement these factors with modern performance fish rockers and staged-bottom contours. We knew that to give the board the level of performance that we were looking for , we would need a drastic design attribute to bring the board to life, so we incorporated an element called the “null wing” to reduce surface area and create an intricate angular rail line break which gives the SUPERNOVA it’s unique pivot/point of release. The null wing isn’t quite a wing or a bump—it’s a modern evolution of the original STING, the Swing Twin, and the High Bump Thruster. It cuts surface area, disrupts water flow, and creates a distinct break in the rail line for crisp, immediate release. The SUPERNOVA is aimed at disrupting the standards that kind of work in smaller conditions giving surfers an edge with a compact HP groveler meant for speed and spontaneous maneuvers -- in small waves ONLY.

models.official_description: If the SUPERNOVA was the explosive beginning—a burst of high-performance groveler energy—then the SUPERNOVA PRO is its next evolution: the fully formed force born from that same cosmic core. Built from the SUPERNOVA’s blueprint, the SUPERNOVA PRO morphs its signature null wing and rebalances the elements that made its predecessor a standout—creating a more centered, versatile feel. Think elemental rebirth, engineered for everyday performance. The SUPERNOVA PRO was made to coexist with the SUPERNOVA—commanding the space where grovelers start to lose grip. When the waves step up from small to solid, the SUPERNOVA PRO steps in to harness that energy.

models.official_description: Built from the cosmic core of the SUPERNOVA system, the SUPERNOVA TWIN takes the same high-performance blueprint and mutates it into a more explosive, free-flowing form. Where the original SUPERNOVA focused on compact control through a multi-fin platform, the TWIN strips everything back and taps into pure rail speed, spontaneous release, and instinctive surfing. But unlike traditional twin fins or retro fish outlines, the SUPERNOVA TWIN was intentionally designed with more curve through the outline so at first glance, it feels familiar. More like the daily driver thrusters high-performance surfers already trust under their feet. The mission was simple: combine the relatability and confidence of a modern HP shortboard outline with design elements specifically tuned to harness the raw speed and freedom of a twin fin. The straight rail line, staged bottom contours, and signature SUPERNOVA DNA remain at the heart of the design. But because twin fins are inherently loose and skatey, we softened the aggressive null wing into more of a refined bump maintaining the essence and identity of the SUPERNOVA family while adding smoother transition and more controlled release through turns. That subtle bump still acts as the identifier. The fingerprint of the system. And then there’s the tail. A squash-tail twin-fin-only shortboard is practically unheard of. Even Noel Salas was caught off guard when he first unboxed it. But once the board hit the water, the feeling completely flipped the twin and thruster worlds upside down. The speed and freedom of a twin with the confidence and familiarity of a performance shortboard. Fast pumps, full rail engagement, tail-high drift, and spontaneous direction changes become second nature. This is not a retro nostalgia twinny. This is alternative performance evolution supercharged through the SUPERNOVA lens.

models.official_description: The Bishop stands as a symbol of balance, conviction, and composure — a dependable daily driver. It has long been a cornerstone of AIPA design, representing power under control — the meeting point between precision and flow.. In Scripture, a bishop is an overseer — one who protects, guides, and upholds what is sacred. That spirit of guardianship runs deep in this design. The Bishop was shaped to command respect in the water, not through aggression, but through mastery — a board for surfers who understand that true power is best expressed through control. Designed for Joel Bishop and refined for today’s performance surfer , The Bishop became Kolby Aipa’s go to board for Huntington Beach, a spot that has a diverse range of wave shapes. The Bishop features a clean, modern round tail outline with roll-single-double-vee + double-staged contours and medium rails that engage smoothly from takeoff through the turn. Its rocker is tuned for drive and projection, giving it the ability to hold high lines with grace and strike with precision when the moment comes. Whether you’re threading the needle in shoulder-high surf or pushing through solid overhead walls, The Bishop feels stable, confident, and connected — a board that rewards focus and flow. It’s a reminder that great work comes from great discipline, and that true leadership — like true surfing — begins with faith, patience, and purpose. models.official_description: The Titan Glider is a modern Hawaiian tribute to something ancient. Inspired by the traditional ?Olo boards—once reserved strictly for Ali?i, Hawaiian noblemen and royalty—this design blends deep heritage with AIPA innovation, in a massive, flowing Sting outline. The ?Olo was the largest of all of ancient Hawaii's surfboards, ranging from 16 to 20 feet in length. It was considered a prized possession—a symbol of status, mastery, and connection to the ocean. The Titan Glider compacts the monstrous size and spirit of the ?Olo into a 10'6"–12'2" range. At first glance, the Titan is pure elegance—long, sleek, and built to fly. But don’t let that fool you. The Sting wing strategically removes surface area in the back end of the board, adding an element of performance—letting you bank turns that would otherwise slip away on a board this size. It paddles like a canoe, glides like a ghost, and reacts when you lean in. This board is for surfers chasing that ancient feeling with modern style—the kind of ride that is about presence. The Titan Glider is more than a board. It’s a return to source. Built with reverence. Designed to capture the essence of royalty.

models.official_description: Taking hints from an experimental board made in 1977 made by Ben Aipa, The Wrecking Ball is the High Performance / Super Groveler that fills the void between Fish and HP Shortboards that brings together all the design qualities needed to pack a ton of action into a small board. The squatty broad outline in the front half of the board gives it paddle/planing dominance while the inclusion of the unmistakable Aipa STING (wing) abruptly breaks the outline to bring in a pulled in performance hip curve that meets a full tail section with a double pronged bat tail that feels like a combo of swallow and squash tails for lift and bite allowing the board to be highly responsive for tight radius turns without sacrificing drive. Updated with high-performance bottom contours, the Wrecking Balls main feature is the hydroplane speed boat inspired “Bevel Rails”. This 45 degree split rail design element provides increased lift, rail to rail reduced surface area for heightened performance, and positive rail control for carves.. When the rest of the crowd is struggling on their standard shortboards, the Wrecking Ball in Surftech/ Fusion HD epoxy construction, will give you the power to drive down the line with unparalleled speed and bash through sections without remorse (with a devilish grin on your face..) Target Sizing: 2-5” below standard shortboard with 1-3 L added volume depending on skill level. Wave range: Below average to above average/ waist high to overhead. Use in conditions you would either use a groveler or ditch your shortboard on good days for a new sensation. Fin Configuration: Quad on average basis to overhead, switch to thruster for over head high for added holding power.. Suggested Fins: Primary/(NVS) Horseman Quads, Secondary/(NVS) Hook Quads for juicy surf, Alternative/(NVS) Sequencer Quads for small days

models.official_description: ACF SIGNATURE AIRBRUSH OPTIONS: The Alan Cleland File, a performance-driven model developed in collaboration with ISA World Champion Alan Cleland Jr. Inspired by Alan’s feedback on our IF15 model, this board takes all the speed and forgiveness of the IF15 and tailors it for surfers who demand precision and control in critical sections. ​ The original IF15 is built for flat-out speed on open faces and aerials with its flatter deck design, spreading volume out to the rails. With the Alan Cleland File, we’ve moved to a dome deck, concentrating the board’s thickness along the stringer and reducing rail volume. This refined shape allows for seamless rail-to-rail transitions, providing riders with sharper control and enhanced carving ability in steeper, more technical parts of the wave. ​ Ideal for surfers who want a responsive, nimble feel underfoot, the Alan Cleland File delivers power and versatility. With a sleek, narrower outline and thinner rails, this board thrives in the pocket, making tight turns and deep carves feel natural and precise. Retaining the IF15’s single concave and medium rocker, the Alan Cleland File strikes a balance between speed and maneuverability that’s versatile across a variety of conditions. Offered in Polyurethane, EPS epoxy & stringerless EPS epoxy constructions, the Alan Cleland File is built to meet the demands of surfers who push their limits. Ride the board that’s been refined by one of the world’s top surfers and feel the difference in every wave. ​ "Note: This board features low rails, so it's recommended to order it 1/16 to 1/8 inch thicker than your usual board for optimal performance."

models.official_description: The Italo Ferreira – ALL IN is the latest innovation born from a powerhouse collaboration between legendary shaper Timmy Patterson, Brazilian shaping icons Sylvio “Tico” and Adriano “Teco” from Silver Surf Surfboards, and none other than World and Olympic Champion Italo Ferreira. The name “ALL IN” captures not only the evolution of Italo’s iconic IF15 model but also his fearless, “All or Nothing” mindset as he charged into his 2024 season with. This board became one of Italo’s game-changing tools to achieve his goals. ​ DEVELOPMENT Known for his meticulous approach to board design, Italo worked hand-in-hand with the T.Patterson and Silver Surf teams to shape the ALL IN. After making waves in the surfing world with his celebrated IF15, Italo sought to fine-tune his craft by experimenting with a variety of board designs. Taking inspiration from both T.Patterson and Silver Surf designs, he cherry-picked the features that best matched his explosive style. The result? A board that embodies Italo’s vision for progression. Its performance has already been proven with his emphatic victory at the 2024 Brazilian WSL stage in Saquarema and a runner-up finish at the WSL Finals at Trestles. ​ DESIGN FEATURES Outline: The ALL IN features a slightly narrower nose and tail compared to the original IF15, giving it a sleek, knife-like shape. This design enhances looseness while delivering excellent drive in turns. Bottom Contour: A medium concave flows through the board’s center, transitioning into a smooth double concave around the fins. This setup amplifies speed, rail-to-rail transitions, and precision control in critical maneuvers. Rails: Refined rails, especially near the tail, improve control and responsiveness at high speeds, making it an ideal choice for powerful, fast-paced surfing. Rocker: The pronounced nose and tail rocker allow the board to sit comfortably in the pocket and carve effortlessly through tight sections of the wave. Volume: Concentrated volume through the center enhances paddling power without compromising high-performance characteristics. ​ ​ WHO IS THIS BOARD FOR? The ALL IN is a high-performance shortboard designed for intermediate to advanced surfers who demand precision and control in quality surf. Whether you’re chasing powerful beach breaks or carving on world-class point waves, this board is made to push your surfing to the next level. ​ TAIL OPTIONS AND BUILD MATERIALS The ALL IN is offered with four tail options: Squash, Round, Round Pin, and Swallow Tail. Available in a variety of constructions to match your preferences and performance needs: PU (Polyurethane) EPS Epoxy Stringerless Carbon Epoxy Builds

models.official_description: The FMTR was developed hand-in-hand with Timmy Reyes, shaped and refined swell by swell over the past year with one clear goal: create a shorter, more performance-driven board that can handle steep, hollow, powerful surf without forcing the rider into a longer step-up. ​ The outline features a more high-performance feel while still maintaining a fuller, user-friendly nose. This allows for confidence on late, steep drops while keeping enough volume under the chest to paddle into bigger days without sacrificing wave count. ​ The rails have been lowered and refined compared to traditional mid-volume designs — not extreme, but tuned just enough to provide better bite, control, and hold in critical sections. This rail profile, combined with the pulled-in tail, keeps the board locked in on fast, hollow faces and prevents excess volume from getting in the way during hard turns or late takeoffs. ​ The standard tail is a round pin tail, offering smooth rail-to-rail transitions and control at speed & even for more powerful or hollow conditions. ​ Designed with versatility in mind, the FMTR performs exceptionally well as a thruster, but Timmy also fine-tuned it to work surprisingly well as a twin + trailer, making it equally fun and fast on smaller days. This dual-fin personality allows the board to transition seamlessly from punchy beach breaks to lined-up point surf. ​ Timmy typically rides the FMTR about 2 inches shorter than his standard shortboard, keeping it lively and responsive, but it can easily be sized up a few inches depending on wave size and personal preference. ​ The FMTR is a performance-driven design built for surfers who want: ​ A shorter board that paddles well in solid surf Control and hold on steep, hollow drops High-performance surfing without excess tail volume A board that adapts across a wide range of conditions ​ From powerful wedges to lined-up points and even fun days when paired with alternative fin setups, the FMTR is a refined, modern performance board designed to push limits without compromise. ​ **Fun Fact: Timmy Reyes doesn't leave home without it, its always in the truck!

The Synthetic 84 has a lower rocker and the bottom runs flat to a slight single into a slight double and fades into vee off the tail. The deck stays flat with a down rail and beak nose. This allows for maximum volume, yet the down rail lets you still sink this higher volume board onto rail in critical sections and off your bottom turns. It has a fuller outline so this board likes to increase your wave count and really comes in handy battling out the every day crowds. You will want to ride this model about 2 inches shorter than your everyday shortboard and comes in a tri fin set up. * With its flat deck design, this board extends foam all the way to the rail, resulting in a high volume board. As a result, some customers may find it necessary to order this board 1/16 to 1/8 inch thinner than their usual preference in order to achieve their desired volume.
